tweets.text: RT @DHSgov: The World Cup is coming to a close and a champion will be crowned TODAY! We want EVERYONE to enjoy the final as safely as possible. It’s up to us to report signs of suspicious activity to local authorities. Play your part. Stay Alert. Speak up. Below is some guidance all fans should follow: ✅Be sure to arrive to the stadium early as there will be increased security and long security lines. ✅Bring plenty of water. ✅Whether you are at the final match or at a watch party, stay aware of your surroundings and be vigilant. ✅Remember: If you see something, say something. Report suspicious activity to local authorities. Together, we can keep the game safe for EVERYONE.

tweets.text: If something is genuinely decentralized, it should not be regulated like a bank. Getting that distinction right took years of work, and we finally have the opportunity to make it law. Let’s pass the Clarity Act.

tweets.text: RT @RepStutzman: I was honored to join @FinancialCmte Chairman @RepFrenchHill and @RepBryanSteil at the field hearing on the anniversary of the CLARITY Act passing the House. As @RepTimMoore, @RepTimmons, @RepHaridopolos, @RepMeuser, and I all discussed on this panel at Federal Hall, the CLARITY act is a crucial piece of legislation to secure our economic future. Now, the senate needs to pass CLARITY!

tweets.text: One year ago, the GENIUS Act became law. The GENIUS Act was an important first step in securing the dollar’s dominance, but we must capitalize on that momentum if we are going to cement America’s legacy as the crypto capital of the world. Let’s get the Clarity Act done!

tweets.text: No regulation didn’t just mean no oversight. It meant that when Celsius, Voyager, and FTX went bankrupt, customer assets were treated like corporate property instead of customer property. The Clarity Act fixes the rule that let that happen. Protecting customers in bankruptcy shouldn’t be a partisan idea.
